title: Log in and reach dashboard
description: Verifies a returning user can log in with valid credentials and is redirected to the dashboard.

steps.description: Navigate to /login
Fill in the email field with {{LOGIN_USER}}
Fill in the password field with {{LOGIN_PASSWORD}}
Submit the login form
Verify the user lands on the dashboard page and the URL contains /dashboard

title: Create account with target exam and reach dashboard
description: Verifies a new user can sign up with name, email, password, and target exam and is immediately redirected to the dashboard without requiring email confirmation.

steps.description: Navigate to /login
Switch to Sign Up mode
Fill New User Name into the name field
Fill newuser+signup1@example.com into the email field
Fill ValidPassword123! into the password field
Select JEE Advanced from the target exam dropdown
Submit the signup form
Verify the user lands on /dashboard and no email confirmation message is shown

title: Sign out from the sidebar
description: Verifies a signed-in user can sign out from the sidebar and is returned to the login page.

steps.description: Navigate to /login
Fill in the email field with {{LOGIN_USER}}
Fill in the password field with {{LOGIN_PASSWORD}}
Submit the login form
Click the sign out control in the sidebar
Verify the user is redirected to /login and can no longer access protected routes

title: View dashboard overview metrics and quick actions
description: Verifies the dashboard loads with stat cards showing solver sessions, Feynman avg, open mistakes, and the three quick action buttons.

steps.description: Navigate to /login
Fill in the email field with {{LOGIN_USER}}
Fill in the password field with {{LOGIN_PASSWORD}}
Submit the login form
Verify the four stat cards (Solver Sessions, Feynman Avg, Open Mistakes, Focus Hours) are visible
Verify the three quick action buttons — Start Focus Session, Feynman a Concept, Review Mistakes — are all visible and clickable

title: Start a focus session from the dashboard quick action
description: Verifies the Start Focus Session quick action navigates to /focus and shows the PDF upload screen.

steps.description: Navigate to /login
Fill in the email field with {{LOGIN_USER}}
Fill in the password field with {{LOGIN_PASSWORD}}
Submit the login form
Click the Start Focus Session quick action button
Verify the URL is /focus and a PDF upload area or drag-and-drop zone is displayed

title: Submit a physics problem to Socratic Solver and receive Socratic guidance
description: Verifies the solver returns a Socratic (question-based) hint response, not a direct answer or error, and the hint progress bar increments.

steps.description: Navigate to /login
Fill in the email field with {{LOGIN_USER}}
Fill in the password field with {{LOGIN_PASSWORD}}
Submit the login form
Navigate to /solver
Fill 'A ball is thrown vertically upward with velocity 20 m/s. What is the maximum height reached? (g=10 m/s²)' into the problem input area
Send the problem to the solver
Verify the AI response is visible, is phrased as a guiding question or hint (Socratic style), does not directly give the numeric answer, and the hint progress bar shows at least 1 of 5 filled

title: Navigate across all main sections from the sidebar
description: Verifies a signed-in user can navigate via the sidebar to every main section — focus, feynman, solver, mistakes, problems, profile, dashboard — and each page loads.

steps.description: Navigate to /login
Fill in the email field with {{LOGIN_USER}}
Fill in the password field with {{LOGIN_PASSWORD}}
Submit the login form
Click the Focus link in the sidebar
Click the Feynman link in the sidebar
Click the Solver link in the sidebar
Click the Mistakes link in the sidebar
Click the Problems link in the sidebar
Click the Profile link in the sidebar
Click the Dashboard link in the sidebar
Verify the dashboard page is displayed with stat cards and quick actions visible

title: Complete Feynman evaluation from topic to scored results
description: Verifies a user can enter a topic, write an explanation, submit for AI evaluation, and receive a numeric score plus strengths and knowledge gaps.

steps.description: Navigate to /login
Fill in the email field with {{LOGIN_USER}}
Fill in the password field with {{LOGIN_PASSWORD}}
Submit the login form
Navigate to /feynman
Fill 'Newton's Laws of Motion' into the topic field
Click the Next or Proceed button to advance to the explain stage

Fill a 3-sentence explanation: 'Newton's first law states objects stay at rest or in uniform motion unless acted upon by a force. The second law says force equals mass times acceleration. The third law states every action has an equal and opposite reaction.' into the explanation textarea
Click Submit for Evaluation
Verify a numeric score (0-100) is displayed
Verify at least one strength point and at least one knowledge gap or suggestion is listed in the evaluation result

title: Submit answer in focus session and receive AI feedback
description: Verifies that after uploading a PDF and starting a session, submitting an answer to a flashcard prompt returns substantive AI feedback.

steps.description: Navigate to /login
Fill in the email field with {{LOGIN_USER}}
Fill in the password field with {{LOGIN_PASSWORD}}
Submit the login form
Navigate to /focus
Upload the file at c:\Users\Sriniketh\OneDrive\Documents\EurekaAI\testsprite_tests\sample_notes.pdf using the PDF file input
Click Start Session
Wait for at least one flashcard or question to appear
Fill 'This concept relates to the conservation of energy, where the total mechanical energy in a system remains constant if no external non-conservative forces act on it.' into the response textarea
Submit the answer
Verify AI feedback is displayed that is more than one sentence and references the content of the answer or the topic

title: Start focus session after selecting a custom page range
description: Verifies the user can upload a PDF, specify a page range, start the session, and see flashcards generated from that range.

steps.description: Navigate to /login
Fill in the email field with {{LOGIN_USER}}
Fill in the password field with {{LOGIN_PASSWORD}}
Submit the login form
Navigate to /focus
Upload the file at c:\Users\Sriniketh\OneDrive\Documents\EurekaAI\testsprite_tests\sample_notes.pdf using the PDF file input
Set the page range to pages 1 to 2
Click Start Session
Verify flashcard content or questions appear on screen, indicating the PDF was processed and content was generated

title: Navigate to Solver from sidebar
description: Verifies a signed-in user can click the Solver sidebar link and the solver page loads.

steps.description: Navigate to /login
Fill in the email field with {{LOGIN_USER}}
Fill in the password field with {{LOGIN_PASSWORD}}
Submit the login form
Click the Solver link in the sidebar
Verify the solver page is displayed with a problem input area

title: Reject signup when name is missing
description: Verifies the signup form shows a validation error when the name field is left empty.

steps.description: Navigate to /login
Switch to Sign Up mode
Fill newuser+missingname@example.com into the email field
Fill ValidPassword123! into the password field
Leave the name field empty
Submit the signup form
Verify a validation error message is shown and the user stays on the login page

title: Start a Feynman exercise from dashboard quick action
description: Verifies a user can click the Feynman quick action on the dashboard and land on the Feynman topic entry screen.

steps.description: Navigate to /login
Fill in the email field with {{LOGIN_USER}}
Fill in the password field with {{LOGIN_PASSWORD}}
Submit the login form
Click the Feynman a Concept quick action button
Verify the Feynman page is loaded and a topic input field is visible

title: Use hint chips to advance Socratic guidance
description: Verifies clicking a hint chip in the solver sends a follow-up request and the solver responds with a deeper nudge while the hint progress bar increments.

steps.description: Navigate to /login
Fill in the email field with {{LOGIN_USER}}
Fill in the password field with {{LOGIN_PASSWORD}}
Submit the login form
Navigate to /solver
Fill 'Explain how a capacitor charges in an RC circuit' into the problem input area
Send the problem to the solver and wait for the AI response
Click the first available hint suggestion chip
Verify a new AI response appears in the chat that is different from the first response, and the hint progress bar shows more than 0 filled segments

title: Create a problem, search for it, launch solver, then delete it
description: Verifies the full problem bank CRUD flow — add a problem, find it via search, open the solver from it, return, and delete it.

steps.description: Navigate to /login
Fill in the email field with {{LOGIN_USER}}
Fill in the password field with {{LOGIN_PASSWORD}}
Submit the login form
Navigate to /problems
Click the Add Problem button
Fill 'TC Work Energy Theorem' into the title field
Fill 'Physics' into the subject field
Select Hard as the difficulty
Fill 'NCERT' into the source field
Fill 'A block of mass 5kg is pushed along a frictionless surface. Find the work done by the applied force over 10 meters.' into the statement field
Save the problem
Search for 'TC Work Energy' in the search input
Click on the problem to open its detail panel
Click Solve with AI to launch the solver
Click the Back button to return
Delete the problem from the detail panel or list
Verify 'TC Work Energy Theorem' no longer appears in the problems list

title: Navigate between flashcards in an active focus session
description: Verifies the user can use Next and Previous controls to cycle through flashcards generated from a PDF.

steps.description: Navigate to /login
Fill in the email field with {{LOGIN_USER}}
Fill in the password field with {{LOGIN_PASSWORD}}
Submit the login form
Navigate to /focus
Upload the file at c:\Users\Sriniketh\OneDrive\Documents\EurekaAI\testsprite_tests\sample_notes.pdf using the PDF file input
Click Start Session and wait for flashcards to load
Click the Next flashcard button
Click the Previous flashcard button
Verify the flashcard content changes when navigating and the Previous button returns to the first card content

title: Create, review detail, and delete a mistake entry
description: Verifies the full mistake journal CRUD — create a mistake with all fields, view its detail, delete it, and confirm it's gone.

steps.description: Navigate to /login
Fill in the email field with {{LOGIN_USER}}
Fill in the password field with {{LOGIN_PASSWORD}}
Submit the login form
Navigate to /mistakes
Click the Add Mistake button
Fill 'Math' into the subject field
Fill 'Integration by parts' into the topic field
Fill 'Forgot to apply the formula in the correct order — chose u and dv incorrectly' into the what-went-wrong field
Fill 'Use LIATE rule to choose u: Logarithm, Inverse trig, Algebraic, Trig, Exponential' into the correct-approach field
Save the mistake
Click on the newly created mistake to open its detail view
Delete the mistake
Verify the 'Integration by parts' mistake entry is no longer visible in the mistakes list

title: Sidebar remains functional after visiting multiple sections in one session
description: Verifies the sidebar stays present and all links remain clickable after navigating through problems, solver, profile, and back.

steps.description: Navigate to /login
Fill in the email field with {{LOGIN_USER}}
Fill in the password field with {{LOGIN_PASSWORD}}
Submit the login form
Click the Problems link in the sidebar
Click the Solver link in the sidebar
Click the Profile link in the sidebar
Click the Mistakes link in the sidebar
Verify the mistakes page is rendered and the sidebar is still visible with all navigation links present

title: Return to dashboard from solver via back button
description: Verifies the Back button in the solver header navigates the user to the dashboard without losing session context.

steps.description: Navigate to /login
Fill in the email field with {{LOGIN_USER}}
Fill in the password field with {{LOGIN_PASSWORD}}
Submit the login form
Navigate to /solver
Fill 'What is the principle of superposition in waves?' into the problem input
Send the problem and wait for AI response
Click the Back button in the solver header
Verify the user is on /dashboard and the dashboard renders correctly with stat cards visible

title: Update target exam from profile and verify it persists after reload
description: Verifies that changing the target exam in profile settings, saving, and reloading the page shows the updated exam selection.

steps.description: Navigate to /login
Fill in the email field with {{LOGIN_USER}}
Fill in the password field with {{LOGIN_PASSWORD}}
Submit the login form
Navigate to /profile
Change the target exam dropdown to NEET
Click Save to persist the profile changes
Reload the page
Verify the target exam shown in the profile dropdown is NEET after reload

title: Answer state persists across flashcard navigation in focus session
description: Verifies that a typed (unsent) answer is preserved in the response area when navigating to the next card and back.

steps.description: Navigate to /login
Fill in the email field with {{LOGIN_USER}}
Fill in the password field with {{LOGIN_PASSWORD}}
Submit the login form
Navigate to /focus
Upload the file at c:\Users\Sriniketh\OneDrive\Documents\EurekaAI\testsprite_tests\sample_notes.pdf using the PDF file input
Click Start Session and wait for flashcards to load
Type 'AnswerDraft-Persistence-Test' into the response textarea without submitting
Click Next flashcard
Click Previous flashcard to return to the first card
Verify the response textarea still contains 'AnswerDraft-Persistence-Test'

title: Problem bank stats update after adding and deleting a problem
description: Verifies the Total Problems stat card increments on add and decrements on delete.

steps.description: Navigate to /login
Fill in the email field with {{LOGIN_USER}}
Fill in the password field with {{LOGIN_PASSWORD}}
Submit the login form
Navigate to /problems and note the current Total Problems count
Click Add Problem and fill: title='TC Stats Test', subject='Chemistry', difficulty=Easy, source='Test', statement='Test problem for stat validation.'
Save the problem
Verify the Total Problems count has increased by 1
Open the TC Stats Test problem and delete it
Verify the Total Problems count returns to the original value

title: Feynman follow-up questions are interactive after evaluation
description: Verifies that follow-up questions appear after a Feynman evaluation and the user can interact with them to see additional AI guidance.

steps.description: Navigate to /login
Fill in the email field with {{LOGIN_USER}}
Fill in the password field with {{LOGIN_PASSWORD}}
Submit the login form
Navigate to /feynman
Fill 'Ohm's Law' into the topic field and proceed to explain stage
Fill 'Ohm's law states that the current through a conductor is directly proportional to the voltage across it, given constant temperature. Mathematically, V equals IR.' into the explanation textarea
Submit for evaluation and wait for results
Click on a follow-up question or probe if one is listed
Verify new content appears — either an expanded answer or an AI-generated follow-up explanation — that is different from the initial evaluation result

title: Focus session blocks start without PDF upload
description: Verifies the Focus Session cannot be started without uploading a PDF and shows an appropriate validation or disabled state.

steps.description: Navigate to /login
Fill in the email field with {{LOGIN_USER}}
Fill in the password field with {{LOGIN_PASSWORD}}
Submit the login form
Navigate to /focus
Attempt to click the Start Session button without uploading any PDF
Verify either the Start Session button is disabled, or a validation message is shown indicating a PDF is required

title: Create a mistake and verify all fields are displayed in detail view
description: Verifies all four mistake fields (subject, topic, what went wrong, correct approach) are visible in the detail view after creation.

steps.description: Navigate to /login
Fill in the email field with {{LOGIN_USER}}
Fill in the password field with {{LOGIN_PASSWORD}}
Submit the login form
Navigate to /mistakes
Add a new mistake with subject='Physics', topic='Kinematics', what-went-wrong='Mixed up average and instantaneous velocity', correct-approach='Use v=dx/dt for instantaneous velocity'
Save the mistake and open it from the list
Verify the detail view shows all four fields: subject (Physics), topic (Kinematics), what went wrong, and correct approach with their saved content
